outputs.document.md:
# Table 14-2.03 Exposure Documentation

## Table Title
Summary of Exposure (Safety Population)

## Purpose
This table summarizes the extent of exposure to study treatment, including treatment duration, dose compliance, and treatment intensity for each treatment group in the Safety Population.

## Data Source
- **ADSL** (Subject-Level Analysis Dataset) with exposure variables derived from SDTM EX (Exposure) domain
- Key ADSL variables used:
  - TRTSDT (Date of First Exposure to Study Treatment)
  - TRTEDT (Date of Last Exposure to Study Treatment)
  - TRTDUR (Duration of Treatment in Days)
  - EXDOSE (Dose per Administration)
  - EXSTDTC (Start Date/Time of Exposure)
  - EXENDTC (End Date/Time of Exposure)
  - EXADJ (Reason for Dose Adjustment)
  - EXDOSEU (Dose Units)
  - EXROUTE (Route of Administration)
  - EXDOSFRQ (Dosing Frequency per Interval)

## Display Conventions
- **Treatment Duration**: Calculated as (TRTEDT - TRTSDT + 1) days. Presented as n, mean, SD, median, Q1, Q3, min, max.
- **Dose Compliance**: Calculated as (Actual Total Dose Received / Planned Total Dose) × 100%. Presented as n, mean, SD, median, Q1, Q3, min, max. Compliance categories: <80%, 80-100%, >100%.
- **Treatment Intensity**: Calculated as (Actual Dose Intensity / Planned Dose Intensity) × 100%, where Dose Intensity = Total Dose / Treatment Duration. Presented as n, mean, SD, median, Q1, Q3, min, max.
- **By Group**: All statistics presented by treatment group (e.g., Placebo, Low Dose, High Dose) and overall.
- **Missing Data**: Subjects with missing exposure data are excluded from relevant calculations and footnoted.
- **Rounding**: Continuous variables rounded to 1 decimal place; percentages rounded to 1 decimal place.
- **P-values**: If applicable, ANOVA or Kruskal-Wallis test p-value provided for treatment group comparisons.

## Footnotes
1. Safety Population includes all subjects who received at least one dose of study treatment.
2. Treatment duration = (date of last dose – date of first dose + 1).
3. Dose compliance = (actual total dose / planned total dose) × 100%.
4. Treatment intensity = (actual dose intensity / planned dose intensity) × 100%.
5. Planned dose is based on protocol-specified dosing regimen.
6. Subjects with missing exposure data are excluded from the respective analysis.
7. Percentages may not sum to 100% due to rounding.

## Output Structure
```

Summary of Exposure
(Safety Population)

Parameter/Statistic                    Placebo      Low Dose     High Dose     Total
                                       (N=xx)       (N=xx)        (N=xx)       (N=xx)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  n                                    xx           xx            xx            xx
  Mean (SD)                            xx.x (xx.x)  xx.x (xx.x)   xx.x (xx.x)   xx.x (xx.x)
  Median                               xx.x         xx.x          xx.x          xx.x

  Min, Max                             xx, xx       xx, xx        xx, xx        xx, xx

  Mean (SD)                            xx.x (xx.x)  xx.x (xx.x)   xx.x (xx.x)   xx.x (xx.x)
  Median                               xx.x         xx.x          xx.x          xx.x
  Q1, Q3                               xx.x, xx.x   xx.x, xx.x    xx.x, xx.x    xx.x, xx.x
  Min, Max                             xx, xx       xx, xx        xx, xx        xx, xx
  Compliance Category, n (%)
    <80%                               xx (xx.x%)   xx (xx.x%)    xx (xx.x%)    xx (xx.x%)
    80-100%                            xx (xx.x%)   xx (xx.x%)    xx (xx.x%)    xx (xx.x%)

  Median                               xx.x         xx.x          xx.x          xx.x

P-value (Treatment Duration)           x.xxx
P-value (Dose Compliance)              x.xxx
P-value (Treatment Intensity)          x.xxx
```

## Programming Notes
- ADSL records are filtered to include only Safety Population (SAFFL = "Y").
- Treatment duration derived as ADSL.TRTDUR.
- Dose compliance derived using ADSL variables: actual total dose from EX (sum of EXDOSE) and planned total dose from protocol.
- Treatment intensity derived as (actual dose intensity / planned dose intensity) × 100%.
- Categorical compliance categories defined per SAP specifications.
- P-values from ANOVA (parametric) or Kruskal-Wallis (non-parametric) as appropriate.
- Output generated using SAS PROC REPORT or equivalent with ODS RTF/PDF output.
